instructions.instruction: Use `vault-cli fetch "<url>"` to fetch a URL and store its content as a semantic memory
instructions.relevant_when: User shares a URL to documentation, a GitHub issue, a blog post, or reference material
instructions.why_given: The agent must know the exact CLI invocation to fetch and persist URL content

instructions.instruction: Use `--project <id>` to scope the fetched content to a specific project
instructions.relevant_when: The fetched URL is relevant to a specific project context
instructions.why_given: Without scoping, the fetched memory is global and may pollute other project retrieval

instructions.instruction: Fetched content is automatically stored as tier:semantic — no --tier flag is needed
instructions.relevant_when: User asks about what tier a fetched URL is stored as
instructions.why_given: The agent must not manually specify a tier when fetching; the command handles this automatically

instructions.instruction: Content is automatically truncated to 4000 characters — no action required to limit size
instructions.relevant_when: User worries about large documentation pages
instructions.why_given: The agent should reassure users that large pages are handled automatically

instructions.instruction: Trigger vault-cli fetch when the user shares a reference URL that should persist across sessions
original_snippets: user shares doc link, GitHub issue/PR with important context, reference material needed across sessions
instructions.relevant_when: User pastes a URL to an API doc, RFC, GitHub issue, or blog post
instructions.why_given: The agent must recognise when to proactively fetch without being asked explicitly
